content: In the binderfs.c driver, it allocates a major number for binderfs using alloc_chrdev_region and then creates files using init_special_inode, but in the Rust bindings the only place where alloc_chrdev_region is used it seems like the files are created using cdev_alloc instead. I'm having a hard time understanding the difference between these options. Can someone explain how this would be translated into Rust - I'm guessing the bindings would need some change, but I can't tell what form this change would take. (Change to chrdev.rs? A new file in the kernel crate?)
